digest: Today's biggest story is the rapid-fire release cycle for Claude Code, with five versions shipped in recent days adding powerful automation features like recurring prompts and cron scheduling. Meanwhile, controversial reports about Claude AI's role in US military operations against Iran are generating significant debate in the developer community.

stories.headline: Opus 4.6 Now Defaults to Medium Effort for Max and Team Subscribers
stories.summary: Anthropic has updated Claude Code to default Opus 4.6 to medium effort for Max and Team subscribers, positioning it as the optimal balance between speed and thoroughness [4]. The change removes older Opus 4 and 4.1 models from the first-party API, automatically migrating users to the newer version [4].
key_points: Medium effort selected as sweet spot for most tasks
"Ultrathink" keyword re-introduced for high effort when needed
Automatic migration from legacy Opus versions

stories.headline: Mozilla Firefox Security Partnership Announced
stories.summary: Anthropic has announced a security partnership with Mozilla Firefox, though details remain limited in the initial announcement [11].

stories.headline: Claude AI's Role in Iran Military Operations Sparks Debate
stories.summary: Multiple reports indicate Claude AI assisted US military operations against Iran, with Palantir and Anthropic technology allegedly helping target 1,000 sites in 24 hours [13]. The Pentagon has declined to confirm whether AI was used in bombing operations, including attacks on civilian infrastructure [12].
key_points: Reports claim AI helped coordinate rapid-strike operations
Pentagon refusing transparency on AI military applications
Raises questions about AI ethics in warfare

sources.title: Palantir and Anthropic AI helped the US hit 1k Iran targets in 24 hours
Pentagon Refuses to Say If AI Was Used to Bomb Elementary School
Claude AI Helped Bomb Iran. But How Exactly?

stories.perspectives: The tech community is grappling with the implications, with some questioning the ethics of AI in military operations while others debate the technical specifics of how Claude might have been deployed.

stories.headline: Claude Code Adds Recurring Prompts and Automation Features
stories.summary: Claude Code v2.1.71 introduces powerful automation capabilities with the `/loop` command for running prompts at recurring intervals and cron scheduling tools for session-based automation [1]. The update also adds rebindable voice activation keybindings and expands the bash auto-approval command list [1].
key_points: New `/loop` command enables scheduled prompt execution
Voice activation key now customizable via keybindings.json
Enhanced bash command auto-approval for better workflow

stories.headline: Third-Party API Gateway Support Fixes Rolled Out
stories.summary: Claude Code v2.1.70 resolves critical API 400 errors when using custom base URLs and third-party gateways [2]. The fixes address tool search detection issues and model compatibility problems with Bedrock inference profiles [2].
key_points: Fixed proxy endpoint detection for tool references
Resolved custom model identifier compatibility issues
Improved tool schema rendering for empty responses

stories.headline: New Claude API Skill and Remote Control Features Added
stories.summary: Claude Code v2.1.69 introduces the `/claude-api` skill for building applications with the Anthropic SDK and enhances remote control functionality with custom session naming [3]. The update also improves bash mode navigation and numeric keypad support [3].
key_points: New skill specifically for Claude API development
Custom session titles for remote control sessions
Enhanced keyboard navigation in bash mode

stories.headline: Auto-Memory and Batch Processing Commands Launch
stories.summary: Claude Code now automatically saves useful context to auto-memory and introduces `/simplify` and `/batch` bundled commands for streamlined workflows [9]. The system also improves git worktree support and adds environment controls for claude.ai MCP servers [6].
key_points: Automatic context saving with `/memory` management
New `/copy` command for interactive code block selection
Enhanced git worktree configuration sharing

stories.headline: Remote Control Feature Expands to More Users
stories.summary: Claude Code v2.1.58 announces the expansion of Remote Control functionality to a broader user base [10]. Multiple stability fixes have also been implemented, including Windows config file corruption issues and prompt cache improvements [7][8].
key_points: Wider rollout of collaborative remote control features
Windows-specific file system fixes
Performance improvements for prompt suggestions

stories.headline: Developer Calls for Anthropic to Build New Slack Alternative
stories.summary: A Fivetran blog post arguing that Anthropic should create a modern Slack replacement has generated significant community discussion on Hacker News [15]. The post suggests Anthropic's AI capabilities could revolutionize workplace communication tools [15].
key_points: Proposes AI-native approach to team communication
Critiques current limitations of existing platforms
Generated substantial developer community engagement

sources.title: Anthropic, please make a new Slack

stories.perspectives: The developer community appears divided between excitement about AI-powered collaboration tools and skepticism about whether Anthropic should expand beyond its core AI research focus.
